title: MSVC: error C2010: '.': unexpected in macro parameter list
body:
Using MSVC 2019, I'm unable to use Verilator because it looks like MSVC isn't accepting some of the macros that are used.

For example, in `verilated_cov.h` `VL_COVER_INSERT` is defined as `#define VL_COVER_INSERT(countp,args...)`.  Is there some reason this macro is missing the comma between `args` and `...`?

```patch
--- a/include/verilated_cov.h
+++ b/include/verilated_cov.h
@@ -62,7 +62,7 @@
 ///             VL_COVER_INSERT(&m_cases[i], "comment", "Coverage Case", "i", cvtToNumStr(i));
 ///     }

-#define VL_COVER_INSERT(countp,args...) \
+#define VL_COVER_INSERT(countp,args,...) \
     VL_IF_COVER(VerilatedCov::_inserti(countp); \
                 VerilatedCov::_insertf(__FILE__, __LINE__); \
                 VerilatedCov::_insertp("hier", name(), args))
diff --git a/include/verilated_dpi.cpp b/include/verilated_dpi.cpp
index d25339de..9f4f9468 100644
--- a/include/verilated_dpi.cpp
+++ b/include/verilated_dpi.cpp
@@ -42,7 +42,7 @@
     VL_FATAL_MT(__FILE__, __LINE__, "", \
                 (std::string("%%Error: Unsupported DPI function: ")+VL_FUNC).c_str())

-#define _VL_SVDPI_WARN(message...) \
+#define _VL_SVDPI_WARN(message,...) \
     VL_PRINTF_MT(message)

 // Function requires a "context" in the import declaration
```
